locked_parameters.graph_SWIG: binary treatment D with potential outcomes (Y(1),Y(0)), observed outcome Y = D Y(1) + (1-D) Y(0), and covariates X satisfying conditional exchangeability (Y(1),Y(0)) independent of D conditional on X
locked_parameters.target_estimand: theta_n = E_Pn[Y(1)-Y(0)] = E_Pn[g_{1,n}(X)-g_{0,n}(X)] along a triangular array of laws P_n
locked_parameters.identifying_assumptions: consistency, conditional exchangeability, positivity with local weak overlap kappa_n <= e_n(X) <= 1-kappa_n, square-integrable outcomes, and cross-fitted nuisance learners for outcome regression g_d, propensity e, and the ATE Riesz representer alpha_n(W)=D/e_n(X)-(1-D)/(1-e_n(X))
locked_parameters.estimator_comparison: the original cross-fit DML ATE score of Chernozhukov et al. (2018), the ATE-specialized locally robust semiparametric GMM score of Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, and Robins (2022), and the automatic debiased ML Riesz-score estimator of Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022)
locked_parameters.frontier_object: point-estimator equivalence is measured after normalization by the exploding efficient standard deviation V_n^{1/2}; studentization equivalence is measured by the ratio-consistency of each named variance estimator relative to V_n

named_assumptions.label: ass:exact-ate
named_assumptions.statement: For each \(n\), consistency holds, \((Y(1),Y(0))\perp D\mid X\), and \(\E_n[Y^2]<\infty\).  The target is \(\theta_n=\E_n[Y(1)-Y(0)]\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:local-overlap
named_assumptions.statement: There is a deterministic \(\kappa_n\downarrow0\) such that \(\kappa_n\le e_n(X)\le1-\kappa_n\) almost surely.  The efficient variance satisfies \(V_n\asymp L_n^2\), \(L_n\to\infty\), and \(L_n^2=o(n)\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:common-eif
named_assumptions.statement: For each \(m\in\mathcal M\), \(\sqrt n(\hat\theta_{m,n}-\theta_n)=n^{-1/2}\sum_{i=1}^n \psi_n(W_i)+R_{m,n}\), with \(\max_{m\in\mathcal M}|R_{m,n}|/V_n^{1/2}=o_p(1)\). The expansion uses the same efficient influence function \(\psi_n\) for DML, LRO, and ADML.

named_assumptions.label: ass:eif-clt
named_assumptions.statement: The triangular array satisfies the Lindeberg condition for \(\psi_n\), so \(V_n^{-1/2}n^{-1/2}\sum_{i=1}^n\psi_n(W_i)\Rightarrow N(0,1)\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:variance-linear
named_assumptions.statement: For each \(m\in\mathcal M\), \(\hat V_{m,n}/V_n=1+\Delta_{m,n}+\xi_{m,n}+o_p(1)\), where \(\max_{m\in\mathcal M}|\xi_{m,n}|\to_p0\), and \(\Delta_{m,n}\) is the covariance-kernel projection index defined in Section~\ref{sec:functional}.

named_assumptions.label: ass:kernel-observable
named_assumptions.statement: The kernel \(K_n\), the projections \(\Pi_{m,n}\), and \(V_n\) are identified from the observable law of \((Y,D,X)\) and the named variance rule \(m\). If \(\liminf_n\Delta_{m,n}>0\), then \(\hat V_{m,n}/V_n\) has a nondegenerate limit strictly larger than one for that \(m\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:fixed-overlap
named_assumptions.statement: There is an \(\varepsilon>0\) such that \(\varepsilon\le e_n(X)\le1-\varepsilon\) for all \(n\). In addition, \(\sup_n\E_{P_n}[Y^2]<\infty\), the usual DML product-rate remainder is \(o_p(1)\) after \(\sqrt n\) scaling, and each named variance estimator is ratio-consistent for \(V_n\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:known-propensity
named_assumptions.statement: The propensity score \(e_n\) is known and DML, LRO, and ADML use the same empirical efficient score for variance estimation. Equivalently, the variance-rule adjoint range \(\mathcal{A}_{m,n}\) contains \(\alpha_n\) for every \(m\in\mathcal M\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:two-cell
named_assumptions.statement: The covariate support contains two cells \(x_A,x_B\) with positive probabilities, \(e_n(x_A)=\kappa_n\), \(e_n(x_B)=1/2\), bounded residual variances \(\sigma_A^2,\sigma_B^2\in[c,C]\), and outcome regressions chosen so that \(V_n\asymp\kappa_n^{-1}\). In the \((q_A,q_B)\) basis, the first-order variance-rule adjoint matrices are \(B_{\mathrm{DML},n}=(0,1)^\transpose\), \(B_{\mathrm{LRO},n}=(\lambda,1)^\transpose\), and \(B_{\mathrm{ADML},n}=I_2\).

conjectures.label: student-frontier
conjectures.statement:
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:exact-ate}--\ref{ass:kernel-observable}, including the standardized EIF central limit condition in Assumption~\ref{ass:eif-clt}, the centered point estimators are uniformly equivalent:
\[
  \max_{m,m'\in\mathcal M}|Z_{m,n}-Z_{m',n}|\to_p0.
\]
For any \(m,m'\in\mathcal M\), their studentized statistics are asymptotically equivalent,
\[
  T_{m,n}-T_{m',n}\to_p0,
\]
if and only if
\[
  \Delta_{m,n}-\Delta_{m',n}\to0.
\]
If \(\Delta_{m,n}\to\delta_m\) and \(\Delta_{m',n}\to\delta_{m'}\) with \(\delta_m\ne\delta_{m'}\), then the studentized statistics have different scale limits:
\[
  T_{m,n}\Rightarrow N(0,(1+\delta_m)^{-1}),\qquad
  T_{m',n}\Rightarrow N(0,(1+\delta_{m'})^{-1}),
\]
even though their centered point estimates share the same \(N(0,1)\) limit after normalization by \(V_n^{1/2}\).  In the canonical ordering where ADML absorbs the full weak-overlap Riesz direction and DML/LRO leave progressively larger residual projections, \(\delta_{\mathrm{ADML}}=0<\delta_{\mathrm{LRO}}<\delta_{\mathrm{DML}}\), so Wald intervals separate first through studentization, not through the point estimate.  Moreover, a single estimator \(m\) has ratio-consistent studentization relative to the efficient variance \(V_n\) if and only if \(\Delta_{m,n}\to0\).

conjectures.label: two-cell-witness
conjectures.statement:
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:exact-ate}--\ref{ass:kernel-observable} and \ref{ass:two-cell}, there is a nonempty open set of finite-support triangular arrays for which
\[
  \Delta_{\mathrm{ADML},n}=0,\qquad
  0<\liminf_n\Delta_{\mathrm{LRO},n}
  \le \limsup_n\Delta_{\mathrm{LRO},n}
  < \liminf_n\Delta_{\mathrm{DML},n}.
\]
On this open set,
\[
  \max_{m,m'\in\mathcal M}|Z_{m,n}-Z_{m',n}|\to_p0
\]
but the three Wald half-lengths based on \(\hat V_{m,n}^{1/2}/\sqrt n\) have strict limiting ratios.  The witness is nondegenerate on the observable law: both treatment states occur with positive probability in each \(n\), residual variances are bounded away from zero on the weak-overlap cell, and the strict gaps persist under sufficiently small perturbations of the two cell probabilities and residual variances.  For the displayed two-cell matrices, the indices are computed directly as
\[
  \Delta_{\mathrm{ADML},n}=0,\qquad
  \Delta_{\mathrm{DML},n}=a_{A,n}^2/V_n,\qquad
  \Delta_{\mathrm{LRO},n}
  =
  \frac{(a_{A,n}-\lambda a_{B,n})^2}{(1+\lambda^2)V_n},
\]
so strict separation follows whenever \(0< (a_{A,n}-\lambda a_{B,n})^2/(1+\lambda^2)<a_{A,n}^2\) eventually.
Because \(a_{B,n}/a_{A,n}\to0\), the displayed LRO index converges to \(\eta_A/(1+\lambda^2)\) while the DML index converges to \(\eta_A\), giving strict separation for every fixed \(\lambda>0\).

supporting_theorems.label: fixed-overlap
supporting_theorems.statement:
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:exact-ate}, \ref{ass:common-eif}, \ref{ass:eif-clt}, and \ref{ass:fixed-overlap},
\[
  Z_{\mathrm{DML},n}-Z_{\mathrm{LRO},n}\to_p0,\qquad
  Z_{\mathrm{DML},n}-Z_{\mathrm{ADML},n}\to_p0,
\]
and
\[
  T_{\mathrm{DML},n}-T_{\mathrm{LRO},n}\to_p0,\qquad
  T_{\mathrm{DML},n}-T_{\mathrm{ADML},n}\to_p0.
\]
Thus, in the fixed-overlap product-rate corner, the three named estimators give the same first-order Wald inference for \(\theta_n\).
supporting_theorems.proof_tex:
\begin{theorem}[Theorem 1: fixed-overlap equivalence sanity theorem]\label{thm:fixed-overlap}
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:exact-ate}, \ref{ass:common-eif}, \ref{ass:eif-clt}, and \ref{ass:fixed-overlap},
\[
  Z_{\mathrm{DML},n}-Z_{\mathrm{LRO},n}\to_p0,\qquad
  Z_{\mathrm{DML},n}-Z_{\mathrm{ADML},n}\to_p0,
\]
and
\[
  T_{\mathrm{DML},n}-T_{\mathrm{LRO},n}\to_p0,\qquad
  T_{\mathrm{DML},n}-T_{\mathrm{ADML},n}\to_p0.
\]
Thus, in the fixed-overlap product-rate corner, the three named estimators give the same first-order Wald inference for \(\theta_n\).
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
For any \(m\in\mathcal M\), Assumption~\ref{ass:common-eif} gives
\[
  Z_{m,n}=V_n^{-1/2}n^{-1/2}\sum_{i=1}^n\psi_n(W_i)+R_{m,n}/V_n^{1/2}.
\]
The leading empirical-score term is common to DML, LRO, and ADML, while the remainder term is \(o_p(1)\) uniformly over \(m\).  Therefore, for each pair \(m,m'\),
\[
  Z_{m,n}-Z_{m',n}=(R_{m,n}-R_{m',n})/V_n^{1/2}=o_p(1),
\]
which gives the two displayed centered-statistic differences.  Assumption~\ref{ass:eif-clt} also makes each \(Z_{m,n}\) tight and asymptotically standard normal.

By Assumption~\ref{ass:fixed-overlap}, each named variance estimator is ratio-consistent for \(V_n\): \(\hat V_{m,n}/V_n\to_p1\).  Hence
\[
  T_{m,n}=Z_{m,n}\left(\frac{V_n}{\hat V_{m,n}}\right)^{1/2}=Z_{m,n}\{1+o_p(1)\}.
\]
For any pair \(m,m'\),
\[
  T_{m,n}-T_{m',n}
  =(Z_{m,n}-Z_{m',n})\left(\frac{V_n}{\hat V_{m,n}}\right)^{1/2}
  +Z_{m',n}\left[\left(\frac{V_n}{\hat V_{m,n}}\right)^{1/2}-\left(\frac{V_n}{\hat V_{m',n}}\right)^{1/2}\right].
\]
The first term is \(o_p(1)\) by centered-statistic equivalence and ratio consistency.  The second term is \(o_p(1)\) because \(Z_{m',n}=O_p(1)\) and both bracketed multipliers converge in probability to one.  Taking \((m,m')=(\mathrm{DML},\mathrm{LRO})\) and \((\mathrm{DML},\mathrm{ADML})\) proves the studentized equivalences.\end{proof}

substrate_survey.method: lean_local_search and lean_leansearch only; no Lean source files were opened or cited
results: lean_local_search returned no project-local declarations for Identifiable, Adjustment, Backdoor, ATE, Frontdoor, LATE, or SWIG under the CausalSmith project root.
lean_leansearch for ATE identification returned Mathlib conditional probability, conditional independence, and conditional expectation primitives rather than an AutoID exact-ID DML abstraction.
substrate_survey.bypass_justified: Stage 0.0 is a prose exact-ID setup over triangular arrays and variance-rule projection indices; no existing AutoID Lean primitive was selected for the mathematical setup contract.
